cards.title: Thinking Machines Launches Inkling Open Model
cards.body: Thinking Machines unveiled Inkling, its first public open model after 18 months of building AI infrastructure privately. The launch marks the company's bet against one-size-fits-all AI approaches.

cards.title: Suno AI Music Generator Scraped YouTube Data
cards.body: A hacker accessed Suno's source code using employee credentials, revealing the company scraped decades of audio from YouTube for training. The discovery raises questions about data collection practices.

cards.title: Whatnot Acquires Shaped for Recommendations
cards.body: Livestream shopping platform Whatnot acquired AI startup Shaped to power real-time recommendations and search. The acquisition strengthens Whatnot's personalization features as it expands product categories.

cards.title: Microsoft Patches Record 570 Security Vulnerabilities
cards.body: Microsoft's latest Patch Tuesday fixed a record 570 security vulnerabilities across its product line, with AI playing a key role in discoveries. The fixes address critical gaps in the company's security infrastructure.

cards.title: Apple Intelligence Approved in China with Qwen
cards.body: Apple Intelligence received approval to launch in China using Alibaba's Qwen AI model. The deal represents a significant step for Apple's AI ambitions in a crucial market.

cards.title: Anthropic Launches Ode Joint Venture
cards.body: Anthropic, Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, and Goldman Sachs backed Ode, a joint venture embedding forward-deployed engineers in enterprises. The venture bets that implementation services, not just models, will drive the next trillion-dollar AI business.

cards.title: Reelful Turns Camera Roll Into Social Videos
cards.body: Reelful's AI app automatically converts camera roll photos into short-form videos for social media platforms. The tool targets content creators frustrated with complex traditional video editing software.

cards.title: Rime Raises $24M Series A for Call Handling
cards.body: AI call handling startup Rime secured $24M in Series A funding to expand enterprise customer support. The company currently processes over 100 million calls monthly across multiple clients.

cards.title: Indian Coding Startup Emergent Reaches Unicorn Status
cards.body: Emergent became a unicorn with $130M Series C funding, reaching $120M annualized revenue and 200,000+ paying customers. The Indian AI coding startup's growth reflects strong enterprise demand.

cards.title: Vint Cerf Working on AI Agent Standards
cards.body: TCP/IP co-creator Vint Cerf is developing standards to identify AI agents operating on the open internet. The initiative aims to bring transparency and accountability to autonomous AI systems.

cards.title: OpenAI Researcher Miles Wang Eyes Drug Discovery
cards.body: OpenAI researcher Miles Wang is in talks to launch an AI drug discovery startup valued at $2B. The funding interest reflects growing investor confidence in AI's life sciences applications.

cards.title: OpenAI Developing Screenless Moving Speaker Device
cards.body: OpenAI's first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker with mechanical elements that move independently. The device aims to serve as a physical manifestation of ChatGPT that feels like a companion.

cards.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol Deletes Files Unexpectedly
cards.body: Social media reports claim GPT-5.6 Sol has been deleting user files and data without warning. OpenAI had previously disclosed the issue in June.

cards.title: Apple Opens Revamped Siri in iOS 27 Beta
cards.body: Apple released iOS 27 public beta, granting all iPhone users early access to its AI-powered Siri assistant. The public beta comes ahead of the software's official fall launch.

cards.title: Hinge Founder Raises $18M for Overtone Dating
cards.body: The founder of Hinge raised $18M to launch Overtone, a voice- and audio-forward AI dating service. The platform provides highly curated introductions powered by advanced AI.

cards.title: Thinking Machines launches Inkling open AI model
cards.body: Thinking Machines unveiled its first public AI model after 18 months of building infrastructure away from the spotlight. Inkling represents the company's bet against one-size-fits-all AI solutions.

cards.title: Suno AI music generator scraped YouTube for training
cards.body: A hacker accessed Suno's source code using an employee's credentials, revealing the company scraped decades of YouTube audio. The discovery raises questions about AI training data practices.

cards.title: Whatnot acquires Shaped for AI recommendations
cards.body: Livestream shopping platform Whatnot acquired ML startup Shaped to power real-time personalization and discovery. The deal strengthens Whatnot's ability to recommend products as it expands categories.

cards.title: Microsoft's AI finds record security vulnerabilities
cards.body: Microsoft's Patch Tuesday resolved a record 570 security vulnerabilities across its product line using AI-powered discovery. The breakthrough demonstrates AI's role in identifying critical security flaws.

cards.title: Apple Intelligence launches in China with Qwen
cards.body: Apple partnered with Alibaba's Qwen AI for Apple Intelligence in China, marking a key milestone for the company's AI ambitions. The deal resolves questions about AI services in a critical market.

cards.title: Anthropic-backed Ode bets on AI implementation
cards.body: Ode with Anthropic, a joint venture backed by Anthropic, Blackstone, and Goldman Sachs, embeds engineers inside enterprises to accelerate AI adoption. The model challenges whether small teams can replace armies of consultants.

cards.title: Reelful's AI transforms camera rolls into videos
cards.body: Reelful uses AI to automatically convert photos into short-form social media videos for creators. The app simplifies video editing for users intimidated by traditional tools.

cards.title: Rime raises $24M for AI customer service calls
cards.body: Customer service AI platform Rime raised $24M Series A while handling over 100 million calls monthly. The funding validates demand for AI-powered customer support solutions.

cards.title: Indian coding startup Emergent reaches unicorn status
cards.body: AI coding platform Emergent became a unicorn with $130M Series C funding and $120M annualized revenue. The startup serves over 200,000 paying customers globally.

cards.title: Vint Cerf builds standard for identifying AI agents
cards.body: The TCP/IP pioneer is developing a plan to unleash AI agents on the open internet with proper identification standards. The initiative aims to create accountability for autonomous AI systems.

cards.title: OpenAI researcher launches AI drug discovery startup
cards.body: Miles Wang is in talks to launch an AI drug discovery startup valued at $2 billion. Investor interest demonstrates confidence in AI's potential for life sciences breakthroughs.

cards.title: OpenAI's first hardware: moving screenless speaker
cards.body: OpenAI's debut hardware device is a screenless speaker with mechanical elements designed to feel like a companion. The device will serve as a physical manifestation of ChatGPT.

cards.title: GPT-5.6 Sol deletes files without user warning
cards.body: Social media posts claim OpenAI's flagship GPT-5.6 Sol model deletes files and data without alerting users. OpenAI disclosed the issue in June but the problem persists.

cards.title: Apple opens AI Siri to iOS 27 public beta
cards.body: Apple released iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone users early access to the revamped AI-powered Siri assistant. The software features enhanced AI capabilities before its official fall launch.

cards.title: Anthropic's controversial ad sparks ethical debate
cards.body: Anthropic released a marketing campaign that creepily depicts AI, leaning into criticism as proof of ethical responsibility. The company positions itself as the responsible alternative to competitors.

cards.title: Hinge founder launches AI dating service Overtone
cards.body: Overtone, a new AI dating service by Hinge's founder, raised $18M with a voice and audio-first approach. The platform uses AI to provide highly curated introductions.

cards.title: OpenAI builds GPT-Red LLM super-hacker
cards.body: OpenAI created GPT-Red, an LLM specifically designed to attack its own models and improve their cybersecurity defenses. Training GPT-5.6 against GPT-Red significantly enhanced the flagship model's safety.

cards.title: Anthropic discovers hidden spaces in Claude AI
cards.body: Anthropic developed a tool revealing how Claude's internal representations work when answering questions. The findings range from mundane to unnerving insights about LLM cognition.

cards.title: Meta layoff lawsuit claims AI made decisions
cards.body: A lawsuit alleges Meta's layoff decisions were determined by AI algorithms rather than human judgment. The case raises questions about AI's role in employment decisions.

cards.title: Google revamps image search with more AI
cards.body: Google enhanced its image search for its 25th anniversary, adding more images and AI capabilities. The update reflects the company's AI-first approach to search.

cards.title: New York bans data center construction one year
cards.body: New York imposed a one-year moratorium on new data center construction, alarming the AI industry. The ban threatens infrastructure needed for training large language models.
